pi_items.source: Task setup_task.sh line 12 confirms this path
Task description and setup_task.sh confirm Slide 3 is the target (index 2)
1 header row + 5 data rows matches task description
Task description specifies 4 columns: Country, Capacity (GW), YoY Growth (%), Global Share (%)
Task description lists these exact headers
China had 393 GW of installed solar capacity at end of 2022 per IRENA data. However, task claims this is from 2023 statistics, which is contradicted. By end of 2023, China had ~609 GW.
USA had 113.1 GW of installed solar capacity at end of 2022. Task claims 2023 data, but 2023 figure was 137.73 GW.
Japan had 78.8 GW at end of 2022. By end of 2023, it was ~87 GW. Actual 2023 value contradicts metadata claim.
Germany had 66.5 GW at end of 2022. By end of 2023, it was ~81.7 GW. Actual 2023 value contradicts metadata claim.
India had 62.8 GW at end of 2022. By end of 2023, it was ~73 GW. Actual 2023 value contradicts metadata claim.
Search for this exact figure returned no matches. China's YoY growth in 2023 new capacity additions was 148% (216.9 GW added), not 28.1%.
Exact figure not found in search results. China's share of global solar capacity in 2023 appears to be much higher (approximately 43% of total global capacity).

pi_items.verified_value: IRENA Renewable Capacity Statistics 2022 (or early 2022 report)
pi_items.source: The capacity values (393, 113, 79, 67, 63 GW) match end-of-2022 IRENA data, not 2023 year-end data. IRENA Renewable Capacity Statistics 2023 (published March 2023) would contain 2022 year-end data. But task description says 2023 data.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real data from IRENA renewable energy reports, but with a critical attribution error. The capacity values for China (393 GW), United States (113 GW), Japan (79 GW), Germany (67 GW), and India (63 GW) are from end of 2022, not 2023. By end of 2023, actual values were significantly higher: China 609 GW, USA 137.73 GW, Japan ~87 GW, Germany ~81.7 GW, India ~73 GW. The YoY growth and global share percentages claimed (28.1% for China growth, 36.7% for China share) could not be verified and appear inconsistent with actual 2023 data. The table structure (6 rows x 4 columns with specified headers) is correctly specified.
